stories.title: RECALL — GOPO Toys Pull String Teething Toys (Choking Hazard, 70,000+ Units, Amazon)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: RECALL: GOPO Toys Pull String Teething Toys — choking hazard. 70,000+ units. Amazon, Aug 2023–Mar 2026. 3 choking incidents. Stop use now. cpsc.gov for details. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting [URL]

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: If you have the GOPO teething toy: cut all silicone strings, write "DESTROYED" on the toy, email photo to recalls@gopotoys.com. Batch numbers: 250905, 250530, 250120, 240315, 231005, 230610 #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting [URL]

article.headline: GOPO Toys Teething Toy Recalled for Choking Hazard — What Colorado Springs Families Need to Know

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
GOPO Toys Teething Toy Recall

#### Image 1A — Social (1200x675)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** Close-up product safety alert scene: a colorful silicone teething toy with pull strings resting on a soft neutral background, warm lighting from above. The toy appears disc-shaped with colorful string tentacles. The bottom third is clean light gray for text overlay. No text, no logos. Bright safety-alert color palette: soft reds and oranges against white. Photorealistic product style, clear and clean.

#### Image 1B — Article Hero (1200x630)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** A calm, informative overhead product scene: colorful silicone teething toys arranged on a clean white background with light natural shadows. Muted pastel safety color tones. Bottom third clean white space for text. No text, no logos. Soft editorial photography style.

---

research.facts:
GOPO Toys Teething Toys Recall
| Claim | Confidence | Source |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| CPSC announced recall June 18, 2026 | HIGH | cpsc.gov official recall page |
| ~70,410 units recalled | HIGH | cpsc.gov, foxbusiness.com, dailyvoice.com |
| Choking hazard: silicone strings smaller and longer than toy safety standard allows | HIGH | cpsc.gov |
| 3 reported incidents of choking or respiratory distress | HIGH | cpsc.gov official |
| Sold on Amazon August 2023–March 2026 | HIGH | cpsc.gov |
| Price: $11–$15 | HIGH | cpsc.gov, foxbusiness.com |
| Batch numbers: 250905, 250530, 250120, 240315, 231005, 230610 | HIGH | cpsc.gov |
| Consumer action: cut strings, write "DESTROYED", photo to recalls@gopotoys.com | HIGH | cpsc.gov |
| CPSC hotline: 1-800-638-2772 | HIGH | cpsc.gov standard hotline |
| Toy description: off-white disc, gray ball center, 6 multi-colored silicone pull strings | HIGH | cpsc.gov |

stories.title: Pikes Peak Library District Summer Adventure — Free, All Ages, Through July 31

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
PPLD Summer Adventure: free for all ages through July 31.

30 days of reading, exploring, creating. Track via Beanstack. Grand prizes include a Broadmoor Cloud Camp stay. ppld.org/summer #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting [URL]

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: PPLD East Library (5550 N. Union Blvd.) has free lunch for kids and teens through July 31, Mon–Fri, plus free books to take home. D11 + PPLD summer partnership. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting [URL]

article.headline: PPLD's Summer Adventure Is Free, It's for All Ages, and It's Running Right Now

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
PPLD Summer Adventure

#### Image 2A — Social (1200x675)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** A bright, inviting summer reading scene: a young child of about 7 years old sitting cross-legged on a colorful library rug, a stack of books beside them, sunlight streaming through a window. Warm golden morning light. Diverse representation. Clean bottom third in soft teal for text overlay. No text, no logos. Friendly, warm illustration-like photography style.

#### Image 2B — Article Hero (1200x630)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** A cheerful summer library scene: children of mixed ages exploring books on low library shelves, warm afternoon light. Bright and airy. Bottom third left clean in soft white/cream for text overlay. No text, no logos. Warm, inviting photography style.

stories.title: D11 Free Summer Meals for All Kids Ages 1–18

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
D11 free summer meals: all kids ages 1–18 in Colorado Springs, no district enrollment needed.

Free breakfast + lunch at schools, community centers, and parks. Find a site: d11.org or kidsfoodfinder.org #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting [URL]

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Any child ages 1–18 in Colorado Springs can eat free this summer — no D11 enrollment, no paperwork. Community meal sites across the city. Find one: d11.org or kidsfoodfinder.org #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting [URL]

stories.title: Free Splash Pads and Spray Grounds Guide for COS Families

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
COS free splash pads this summer:

- John Venezia Park: 10am–6pm
- Panorama Park: 10am–6pm
- Deerfield Hills: 10am–5pm
- Uncle Wilber Fountain (Acacia Park): noon–6pm

Note: Julie Penrose Fountain closed in 2026. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting [URL]

stories.title: New Research Links Excessive Screen Time to Kids' Mental Health Problems

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: New study: excessive screen time linked to anxiety, depression, and ADHD in kids ages 6–17. Physical activity and sleep buffer the effects. AAP 2026: quality and context matter more than strict limits. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting [URL]

article.headline: New Research Confirms Screen Time Links to Kids' Mental Health — And What Actually Helps

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Screen Time and Kids' Mental Health

#### Image 5A — Social (1200x675)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** A thoughtful parenting scene: a parent and child sitting side by side on a couch, watching a tablet together, warm lamp light in the background. The child is about 6 years old, diverse representation. Mood: calm, reflective, not alarming. Soft warm tones. Clean bottom third in soft coral/warm cream for text overlay. No text, no logos. Warm editorial photography style.

#### Image 5B — Article Hero (1200x630)
**Mode:** base_only
**Prompt:** A balanced home scene: a child around age 8 playing outdoors in a backyard, running through green grass, bright afternoon light — contrasted warmly against a soft interior glimpse of a tablet on a couch behind a window. Mood: hopeful, active, positive. Clean bottom third for text overlay. No text, no logos. Bright lifestyle photography style.

research.facts:
Screen Time and Kids' Mental Health Study
| Claim | Confidence | Source |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| Study published in Humanities and Social Sciences Communications (Nature portfolio) 2026 | HIGH | nature.com direct link in search results |
| Data from 50,231 children ages 6–17 (US National Survey of Children's Health 2020-2021) | HIGH | nature.com study |
| Excessive screen time linked to anxiety, depression, behavior/conduct problems, ADHD | HIGH | nature.com |
| Physical activity and sleep mediate the relationship | HIGH | nature.com |
| AAP: no screens before 18 months | HIGH | choc.org (citing AAP guidelines), aap.org |
| AAP: limit to 1 hour/day high-quality content for ages 2–5 | HIGH | choc.org (citing AAP guidelines) |
| AAP 2026 update: prioritize quality, context, conversation over strict time limits | HIGH | choc.org, aap.org |
| Device-free bedrooms and mealtimes recommended | HIGH | aap.org, choc.org |
| 47% of parents' top concern: privacy/safety; 36%: misinformation; 34%: in-person socialization | MEDIUM | Ipsos/Kids Mental Health Foundation survey (2025) via sciencedaily.com |
